seoTitle: Create and Edit Manual Tables With Enter Data in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to use Power BI's Enter Data feature to create and edit manual tables, perfect for custom datasets that don't come from an external source.
intro: Sometimes you need a custom table in Power BI that doesn't come from an external data source. This guide walks through using the Enter Data feature to manually create and edit tables, which is useful for building tailored datasets that fit your specific reporting needs.

steps.heading: Create a new table
steps.body: Open the Enter Data feature in Power BI to start building a table from scratch, defining the columns you need for your dataset.

steps.heading: Enter data manually
steps.body: Input your values directly into the table structure, ensuring each column is filled out consistently and correctly.

steps.heading: Edit existing tables
steps.body: Return to a previously created manual table to update or correct values as your data changes over time.

steps.heading: Explore use cases for manual tables
steps.body: Manual tables are useful for scenarios like custom sales categories or specific customer data logs that don't fit neatly into an automated data source.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for beginners and professionals
steps.body: Keep manual tables organized and clearly labeled so they remain easy to maintain and integrate with the rest of your Power BI model.

faq.question: When should I use Enter Data instead of importing a file?
faq.answer: Enter Data is best for small, custom datasets you need to build from scratch, rather than large datasets that already exist in an external file or source.

faq.question: Can I edit a manual table after it's created?
faq.answer: Yes, you can go back into the Enter Data feature at any time to update, add, or remove entries in an existing manual table.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how much data I can enter manually?
faq.answer: Manual entry is best suited for smaller datasets, since larger volumes are typically better handled through an automated data connection.

faq.question: Can manual tables be combined with imported data in the same report?
faq.answer: Yes, manual tables can be related to and used alongside data imported from other sources within the same Power BI model.
